title: Kubernetes workload security context
description: Per-workload (Deployment / DaemonSet / StatefulSet) effective security-context posture: run-as-non-root, privileged-container, read-only-root-filesystem, allow-privilege-escalation, and host-namespace (hostPID / hostIPC / hostNetwork) flags. Pulled read-only from the Kubernetes API (get/list on apps workloads + core pods). Security-context configuration only — never Secret values, ConfigMap values, container env, or logs. The connector reports a deterministic PASS/FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE verdict per workload; the platform evaluator owns the final policy call.

run_as_non_root.description: Effective runAsNonRoot for the pod template (true only when every container, after pod-level inheritance, runs as non-root).

privileged.description: At least one container requests privileged: true.

allow_privilege_escalation.description: At least one container permits privilege escalation (allowPrivilegeEscalation not set to false).

container_count.description: Number of containers in the pod template (context for interpreting the aggregate flags).
